heading: Transportation or importation of migratory birds; when unlawful
label: 705 Transportation or importation of migratory birds; when unlawful
text: It shall be unlawful to ship, transport, or carry, by any means whatever, from one State, Territory, or district to or through another State, Territory, or district, or to or through a foreign country, any bird, or any part, nest, or egg thereof, captured, killed, taken, shipped, transported, or carried at any time contrary to the laws of the State, Territory, or district in which it was captured, killed, or taken, or from which it was shipped, transported, or carried. It shall be unlawful to import any bird, or any part, nest, or egg thereof, captured, killed, taken, shipped, transported, or carried contrary to the laws of any Province of the Dominion of Canada in which the same was captured, killed, or taken, or from which it was shipped, transported, or carried.
notes: Amendments1969— repealed second par., which prohibited shipment of wild game mammals or parts thereof by any person of the United States to and from Mexico, except by permit from the Secretary of the Interior.Pub. L. 91–1351936—Act , inserted last sentence.June 20, 1936

Effective Date of 1936 Amendment, , provided in part that the amendment by section 4 is effective as of the day aforesaid (). See note under .Act June 20, 1936, ch. 634, § 449 Stat. 1556section 703 of this titleJune 30, 1937
